prompt: We just published a major whitepaper called 'The State of Supply Chain Visibility 2026' -- 40 pages, original survey data from 500 logistics leaders, covers predictive analytics adoption, real-time tracking maturity, and supplier risk scoring. I want to build a 6-week content calendar that squeezes maximum value out of this one asset. Target personas are (1) VP Supply Chain at enterprise manufacturers and (2) Director of Logistics at 3PL providers. Channels: LinkedIn company page, CEO LinkedIn (our CEO was quoted extensively in the report), blog, email to our subscriber list of ~8,000, and one webinar. We have 1 full-time writer with AI assistance. Campaign goal is whitepaper downloads that feed into our ABM nurture. Key stat from the report: '73% of supply chain leaders say they lack real-time visibility into Tier 2+ suppliers' -- that's our headline hook.
expectations: Pipeline generation: the primary CTA across nearly all content pieces is whitepaper download that feeds into the ABM nurture, and the calendar explicitly shows how downloads flow into the nurture sequence -- connecting content output to pipeline entry rather than treating downloads as a terminal metric

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model produces at least 8 derived cluster pieces from the single whitepaper, organized around its three sub-themes (predictive analytics adoption, real-time tracking maturity, supplier risk scoring) so each theme generates multiple pieces across different channels and formats -- demonstrating maximum content output from one 40-page research asset

Content leverage: the headline stat ('73% of supply chain leaders lack real-time visibility into Tier 2+ suppliers') is repurposed as a hook across multiple channels and formats -- LinkedIn posts, blog headlines, email subject lines, webinar promotion -- demonstrating how one data point gets repackaged to drive downloads from different audience touchpoints

Alignment: every content piece is mapped to either the VP Supply Chain at enterprise manufacturers or the Director of Logistics at 3PL providers, with messaging tailored to each persona's distinct priorities -- so the content calendar produces persona-specific assets that sales can use in ABM account outreach

Execution velocity: channel cadence matches sustainable defaults for all five committed channels (LinkedIn company page up to 3x/week, CEO LinkedIn up to 4-5x/week leveraging CEO quotes, blog 1-2x/week, email 1-2x across 6 weeks to the 8K list, exactly 1 webinar mid-campaign as a mid-funnel conversion event) and total weekly volume stays within 6-9 pieces for 1 writer with AI assistance

Pipeline generation: the webinar is structured as a mid-funnel conversion event built around a key whitepaper finding, with a registration CTA that funnels attendees to the whitepaper as a follow-up asset and into the ABM nurture -- not a standalone event disconnected from the download-to-pipeline motion

Execution velocity: production deadlines are set at least 5 business days before every publish date, and the 6-week sequence is ordered so high-leverage pieces (pillar derivatives, webinar promotion, email sends to 8K list) are front-loaded while lower-leverage channel fill is scheduled later -- ensuring the highest-impact content ships first
